name: d1374 Heartbeat Suppress Waiting
goal: Stop the daemon heartbeat from showing "Waiting on Robert" when no real operator action is pending, by backing the display with a deterministic pending_confirmation directive signal plus a tightened classifier prompt.

tasks.name: Tighten the awaiting-operator classifier prompt
tasks.description: Replace _AWAITING_OP_SYSTEM in main.py to distinguish genuine operator decisions from autonomous-system-condition narration, using the confirmed d1362 false-positive as a negative example. Add a regression-documentation test to TestOperatorWaits; no true RED/GREEN cycle applies since the prompt text is not exercised by mocked-grader tests.

steps.description: Replace _AWAITING_OP_SYSTEM (main.py:77-83) with the tightened prompt text distinguishing operator decisions from system-state narration, including the d1362 phrasing as a negative example.

Add test_d1362_style_system_narration_falls_through_when_grader_says_no to TestOperatorWaits in tests/test_main_validate.py, mocking the grader to return awaiting_operator=False for the exact d1362 incident phrasing.

steps.expected: All tests in TestOperatorWaits pass, including the new one.

tasks.name: Add deterministic pending_confirmation signal to the heartbeat
tasks.description: Add _get_pending_confirmation_waits() querying directives with status='pending_confirmation' and merge its result into the waits dict passed to format_heartbeat() from post_heartbeat(), so the WAITING ON section is backed by a real signal in addition to the (now-tightened) classifier.

steps.description: Add `import sqlite3` to tests/test_main_validate.py's imports if not already present, then write test_pending_confirmation_directive_surfaces_in_heartbeat_waits and test_no_pending_confirmation_and_no_operator_waits_suppresses_section in TestOperatorWaits, using an in-memory sqlite3 directives table. NEITHER test mocks format_heartbeat: both assert on the real rendered Slack message via _posts(d) — the first confirms directive-sourced rendering per the design's instruction, the second confirms the WAITING ON section is fully absent (the actual regression guard for the reported symptom).
Run the new tests, expect the surfacing test to fail
steps.expected: test_pending_confirmation_directive_surfaces_in_heartbeat_waits FAILS (directive not yet merged into waits); suppression test may already pass.

steps.description: Implement _get_pending_confirmation_waits() in main.py (placed immediately before post_heartbeat), catching Exception broadly (matching the existing pattern at main.py:2456-2470) so a query failure cannot crash the heartbeat. Modify post_heartbeat (lines 2444-2454) to build merged_waits = {**dict(self._operator_waits), **self._get_pending_confirmation_waits()} (deterministic signal merged LAST so it wins over a stale same-id classifier entry) and pass it as waits= to format_heartbeat.

steps.expected: All tests in TestOperatorWaits pass, including both new tests and the existing test_post_heartbeat_passes_waits.

steps.description: Run full test suite for regressions
steps.expected: No new failures; the unrelated [ACTION REQUIRED] daemon-to-Brain nudge flow tests are unaffected.

tasks.name: Restart daemon to deploy the fix, then verify live
tasks.description: Operational step: after Tasks 1 and 2 are reviewed and committed, call restart_daemon(directive_id=1374) so the daemon picks up the new code and directive #1374 is atomically marked completed before the SIGHUP restart, per the d1364 restart-loop-fix convention. No code changes, no test framework applies. allowed_files lists the plan JSON itself only as a harmless non-empty placeholder (schema requires a non-empty list) — no file is actually edited by this task.

steps.description: Confirm Tasks 1 and 2 passed code review and are committed.
Call the restart_daemon MCP tool with directive_id=1374.
steps.expected: Directive #1374 is marked status='completed' in the directives table before the daemon forks/restarts via SIGHUP.

steps.description: Post-restart live verification (manual): over the next 1-2 heartbeat cycles, confirm the WAITING ON section does not appear during an autonomous-only period with no pending_confirmation directive. This is the only real-world check of Task 1's unverifiable-by-unit-test prompt change — do not skip it. If the false-positive pattern recurs with different wording, file a follow-up directive rather than treating this task as failed.
